title: ICON Apartments
text: I lived here for 2 years and enjoyed every bit of it. Jourdan is a great  property manager and she does her best to maintain a great community. Would recommend any UIUC student/staff to live here !
name: Vinayak Bagdi
responseFromOwnerText: I just wanted to say thank you and that this year has not been the same without you guys. I miss the group and think about you guys all the time! I hope everything is going well in the big world for you.

title: ICON Apartments
text: I am making this review after I had moved out a year ago. I enjoyed living my last two years of college here! Jourdan, the building manager at the time, was so sweet and helpful. Any issues or questions that I had, she immediately assisted me. She was always saying hi to residents and was the kindest. ICON will forever my favorite apartment building on campus, thank you for making my time at UIUC memorable!
name: Kimberly Uruchima

title: ICON Apartments
text: My daughter stayed at this apartment building for almost 3 years and had a truly wonderful experience. The property is well-maintained, safe, and in a great location, but what really sets it apart is the management—especially Jordan. She is absolutely the best! Incredibly helpful, responsive, and genuinely caring, Jordan made sure everything ran smoothly and was always there when needed. It's rare to find a building manager who goes above and beyond like she does. Highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a comfortable and well-managed home.
name: Helen Deng

title: ICON Apartments
text: The mailing system is absolutely awful. You can only pick up your mail from 9-5 on a weekday (when most people have work or classes), and sometimes they close early because there is only one person running the office so if they’re sick or have something else going on, you can’t pick up your packages. Also, the rooms have very little sunlight (the interior windows are blocked by massive walls), and a lot of the furniture was scratched or broken. 2 stars because at least the rooms are fairly spacious, but I don’t think it’s worth it.
name: Bob Smith
responseFromOwnerText:
Hey Bob, we would be more than happy to leave your packages out for you on the white shelves. You need only ask. Apologies for being sick today. 

Hope you have a better weekend.

title: ICON Apartments
text:
Jourdan the property manager is really something special. Every time I had the pleasure to see her or read her emails, she was full of life and clearly invested in creating a lovely little space on campus.

Any time a problem propped up I had full confidence it would be resolved quickly and effectively. Apartment was clean, well furnished, and cozy. Location is wonderfully close to campus and the county market. By far my favorite living experience on campus.
name: Hamilton S
responseFromOwnerText: How to make a girl cry while drinking her morning coffee.. Hamilton you sweet sweet soul. Thank you for your kind words. I miss you guys and I hope the adult world is everything you deserve it to be. <3

title: ICON Apartments
text: I’m really happy with my apartment. The application process, the management and the cleanliness made moving in so much easier and stress free. Every day since, I’ve come home feeling really comfortable and content. The furnishings are new, clean and reliable. So happy 🤍
name: Sierra Jensen
responseFromOwnerText: Look at us now.. we handing out plants. We bonding over veggies and amazon. Tis the truest of true <3

title: ICON Apartments
text:
If you are a lucky person and do not anticipate any substantial problems(such as mail theft, withheld deposit ,food theft or horrible neighbors), then I highly recommend this place. It's in a great location and the landlord is seemingly nice in their public announcements. Otherwise, I advise staying someplace else. I personally experienced all within the parentheses during my stay.

[Response: I think you have mistaken me for someone else. I have only ever written this one review. I have however, elaborated on my original review. It was initially ambiguous, and likely unhelpful. After my experience, I decided that I wouldn't remain in Champaign after I graduated. Thus, I will not be able to come in person. This is my other google account and thus would not be in the system. I sent many emails to the property management about these issues. I even left early due to being unable to sleep, so you'd likely be able to find my information, unless many others have experienced what I had. Thank you for your time.]
name: ai “azylexia” s
responseFromOwnerText: Hi Ai, While I appreciate your honesty when it comes to leaving reviews. This is two one star reviews in a two month period. I am not finding you in our system, nor have I had the pleasure of speaking with you in person about these issues. We keep packages in the leasing office to help with the major campus theft issue that is everywhere. I advise anyone ordering food on campus to be ready and waiting for their delivery driver, and neighbors can always be reported to the manager. I think it would be best if we could speak more about these issues in person please feel free to come and speak with me in the office. I just want to make sure I am not missing anything on my end to be sure your stay is as enjoyable as possible.

title: ICON Apartments
text: We asked for leasing for next year. Their office time is 8 - 5. We came around 11, but nobody was here, and we called, nobody answered as well. We waited around 1 pm until she came. When we asked for the leasing, she had a very very bad attitude for no reason!
name: Haofu Li
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Haofu, 

Thank you for taking the time out of your day to leave us a review. Please know that we don't do walk in tours, we have to have an appointment to give our current residents advance notice. There is only one staff member onsite so we apologize if you came at a time when she was out of office. She was also gone at lunch at 1:00 pm today so I'm unsure who you would have spoken to. But please feel free to give us a call so that we can get this cleared up for you. 217-352-7945.

Things do happen unexpectedly, but we responded to both issues promptly and are committed to getting everything fully resolved for you.

text: Charged $100 for phantom electricity for 3 weeks before moving in
name: Soham Phargade
responseFromOwnerText: Hi Soham, thank you for your feedback. Your lease began on August 1st, which means the utilities became your responsibility as of that date. Even if you hadn’t physically transferred into your new unit yet, the appliances and air conditioning in the unit continue to use electricity to maintain the space. You were also the only one in the unit with a lease from 8/1-8/20 therefore, the utilities were not split 4 ways as they are now. We understand how this can be confusing, and we've already discussed this with you, but we are happy to discuss it again anytime in the office.

title: HERE Champaign
text: This really is one of the worst apartments in Champaign. It is so expensive to begin with and you end up paying for amenities that never work and for an "industrial" look (a cheap and unfinished apt). The leasing agents and managers there are notoriously unhelpful. Whether its parking issues, the car elevator breaking, noise issues, keys not working, broken cabinets, etc they do the bare minimum. Instead of checking apts before leasing them back out they will give you a filthy disgusting apt and then blame you for the previous tenants distruction. Even when calling to ask about how to fix this/ prove that it was not me, they ignored calls and sent the charge to debt collectors. All of which occurred 2 years after I had moved out. They claim to check each apt before a new tenant moves in however this is a complete lie and scam to try and charge you more money. Its astonishing how rude and ignorant these people are.
name: Margaret Lovell
responseFromOwnerText: Hello Margaret, I see that you lived with us in 2021-2022. I would like to say we have had many upgrades at HERE since you stayed with us. We upgraded our lock/key system, we have upgraded almost every amenity space in the building, and have been proactive with our elevator maintenance to avoid any issues this past year. We have a great turn process in place to ensure the apartments are in order prior to move-in and also there is a move-in inspection form sent out virtually to allow you to take pictures of anything wrong in the unit so the blame is not on the incoming resident. There is an all new leasing/manager staff in office as well. If you need any assistance, we would be happy to help if you reach out.

title: HERE Champaign
text:
I am rating this place a one star in hopes of this place getting better for future residents and it is impossible for me to rate this place a zero because I have already lost hope for this place.

First, let me talk about the living conditions here. The house for demonstration shows what the opposite of living her would be like. I thought I would get a TV in my STANDARD aerial room, however, the room is so small and looking so cheap that they did not even care to put TVs in my living space. Also, the room is so small as I placed two large boxes there is zero space for me to walk. Honestly, my bedroom feels like a nest for sparrows and I am not exaggerating. The place is cramped and they can't even afford to have a REAL table as my table is pulled out of the closet, and there is no space for another table. Also, please do something about this "integrated" closet, it is not helpful for me to work or move around it. For example, the pulled out table blocks the first layer of the drawers beneath it, and I have to put the table back every single time to grab something in the drawers. Also, the closets' inside structure is filled with problem. The right hand side of it is separated into two horizontal spaces. At first glance this might seem like a good idea for sorting different types of clothes. However, I must say that the design makes it impossible to put even suits and baseball jerseys vertically in it, it is way better to remove the block in the middle or place it lower, far lower than it is now!

Second, the amenities. Some of the amenities are good, the study lounge is never full, the golf simulator seemed to be always occupied, and the play house is ok. But the elevators, AC, and the doors are always needing to be repaired, especially the elevators and the doors, to me they were never functioning constantly. Here's my solution for the elevators and doors, I am going to ask you a question: which way is cheaper, either fixing the doors and elevators once and for all (which is impossible in my opinion, and the cost of fixing them is constant since you always fix them) or change the doors or elevators for better use (which in my opinion saves you money because they are more durable and not needing to be fixed all the friggin time!) Seven months ago, you said that " a water pipe burst over the winter and scheduled to be replaced the week of July 17th," how's that going? Also, "The HERE building is unique as we offer two story units for each unit which means that there is actually 28 stories." that is not the way to handle things like a responsible apartment! How about making the elevator faster so that residents won't have to wait five to ten minutes for a two-story ride on every floor? As of yesterday, there was a "water pipe burst" in the building, and everyone was forced to evacuate from the building. The water is dripping all over the floors and stairs, which led me questioning whether there are some serious issues that requires the help of firefighters.

Third, I would like to ask for some gifts for the residents, not something like a month's rent because that is too much to ask from you guys obviously, but residents like us need something like some community rewards or coupons to a discount for next years rent and I am talking about a discount fo EVERY. SINGLE. MONTH!
name: 李易辰
responseFromOwnerText:
Good Morning!  I am so sorry you have experienced this at HERE Champaign.  The building experienced an unforseen power outage on Monday which led to these other incidents.  We are working with contractors around the clock to ensure all matthers are taken care of.  The elevators were repaired yesterday and the hot water this morning.  As far as the size of your room goes, we actually are upgrading the furniture in the bed spaces.  I am happy to schedule a meeting or a phone call to discuss further.  Please feel free to give us a call at 217-310-4373 or email be directly at aumbarger@articlestudentliving.com.
Sincerly,
HERE Champaign Mangement

text: DO NOT LIVE HERE! The parking is insanely difficult and takes forever. title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I have quite enjoyed my experience living here. The apartment is nice and pretty well sound proofed which is a big thing for me. The maintenance and leasing office staff are nice and get to most problems very quickly. The biggest gripe and why I have lowered the stars even though I love living here is with the parking garage and security concerns.

One of the biggest things is that it is basically impossible to reach the leasing office unless you go in person. The people are helpful if you're able to get to them but any time I have tried calling when the office is open no one picks up. Sometimes the office will close early or not be open at all with no warning. Any time I have tried calling both the phone numbers provided the phone will ring and you'll be connected to their AI assistant Sam which how does that help anyone?? I've never been locked out but it worries me that if I could not get ahold of them for a simple problem then what would happen if I did?

The second is that I've brought up to the office time and time again about the parking garage as I pay a lot of money to park my car in there both for safety of the car and mainly myself. When I toured I was promised the garage would have secured parking gate where you would need a scanner to enter and that sounded great. Not only was the gate not ready before then but it took 3 months with asking the leasing office about it to actually get the gate working. I asked about getting compensation during this time as I was paying for something I wasn't receiving but was rejected. This is also to mention that the gate breaks often and would be left open for anyone to enter or park their car in and by the time of writing this review the gate is STILL BROKEN with no updates from management. This might not sound like a big deal but there have been people smoking in the garage, there have been homeless people camping in there, and I have had things stolen out of the garage to little concern of the management when I reported it to them. Security is a big concern of mine and I wish it was taken more seriously here. Another problem while not too related to security is that there is a million delivery drivers who will park their cars in the garage, in front of the garage, or in the entrance to the parking lot behind the building and they block the way in or out so you cannot leave. While I understand that's not something the management can really fix I'm surprised it's a problem that I deal with weekly and it is very annoying. I'm hoping these are problems that can be fixed in the last few months of me living here but I'm not too hopeful. I feel that the amount I pay to live here these should be non-issues.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
This has been one of the worst apartment experiences I’ve ever had. I strongly do NOT recommend Latitude to anyone.

The lease transfer / subleasing policy is outrageous. If you need to move out early, you are basically forced to lose the equivalent of THREE MONTHS of rent. This is completely unreasonable and absolutely not renter-friendly. What makes it worse is that this was not clearly explained or emphasized at signing — you only find out how bad it is once you’re already stuck.

On top of that, the HVAC system is a disaster. The air conditioning / heating breaks repeatedly during the winter, when you actually need heat to live comfortably. I’ve dealt with constant issues, unreliable fixes, and no permanent solution. Living here during colder months was genuinely miserable.

For the price they charge, this place delivers terrible management, terrible maintenance, and zero accountability. They are happy to take your money, but when problems come up, you are on your own.

Save yourself the stress, money, and frustration. There are far better apartments out there. I regret choosing this place and would never live here again.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I’ve never been more disappointed and infuriated with an apartment management team in my life. Latitude Apartment has shown nothing but greed, hypocrisy, and complete disrespect for its tenants.

First of all, I renewed my lease for 2026–2027, but before that lease even took effect, I asked to cancel it. Guess what? They refused to let me terminate it. When I tried to find a sublease instead, they had the audacity to tell me I’d need to pay three full months of rent just to do so. Three months! Every other apartment I’ve ever heard of charges a reasonable flat sublease fee — usually around $500, not thousands of dollars. Latitude’s policy is nothing short of predatory and exploitative.

As if that wasn’t insulting enough, they recently started advertising a $1250 renewal bonus for the same 2026–2027 term. Naturally, I asked if I could receive it too, since I had renewed earlier — in good faith. Their response? A flat-out no. They said my “benefit” was that my rent wouldn’t increase. But when I asked for the current rent rate, I found out it’s actually lower than what I’m paying now. So not only did I get no bonus, I’m paying more than new tenants. It’s absolutely absurd and completely unfair.

And the way their staff handled everything made it even worse. The attitude was rude, dismissive, and completely unprofessional. One staff member even told me not to come to the office to talk about it and to “just send an email.” But anyone who’s dealt with Latitude knows that emails are almost never answered — you can send ten and be lucky to get one response back.

Latitude Apartment clearly doesn’t care about its residents. They care only about squeezing as much money as possible out of people and hiding behind “policy” whenever they’re confronted. I’m utterly disgusted and deeply regret ever signing a lease with them. If you value fairness, honesty, and basic human decency, stay far away from this place.
name: Charle
responseFromOwnerText: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We appreciate your perspective and understand that your experience may not have fully aligned with your expectations. Our policies are crafted to promote consistency and fairness, though we acknowledge that individual situations can vary. We are dedicated to enhancing our communication and service moving forward. If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out. We are here and ready to assist you! --Latitude Apartments Team

title: Latitude Apartments
text: Honestly, this place is a scam. Management will find every possible way to keep your money. The “security deposit” is basically two months of rent you’ll never get back, and if you try to sublease, get ready to lose around three months’ rent in total. Their renewal deals are a joke — the “bonus” changes all the time with no clear rule. If you renew early, you might miss out on over $1,000 compared to people who wait. The building is super old, nothing gets updated, and if your unit faces the campus side, you’ll never sleep because of motorcycle and car noise all night. Somehow they still charge a $20 “premium view” fee for that noisy view. Save yourself the stress — there are way better places nearby with honest management and quieter surroundings.

title: Latitude Apartments
text: PLEASE READ ENTIRE THING. Latitude apartments is the worst apartment I've ever lived at. All they want is to take your money. The front desk staff are extremely rude and you will pay tooth and nail for the worst conditions. I have a feeling they are BUYING good reviews. They have lost over 10+ of my packages and mocked me in the process. Front desk and management will actively gaslight you into thinking they are not responsible when literally their package system is broken. They will blame anyone but themselves. They do not answer phone calls, rarely answer emails, and can only be reached if you talk to them in person. Even then, they will likely give you an attitude. I have anxiety every time I speak to them. I paid $1900/month for an apartment that was promised to me to be cleaned but was actually broken, full of dog hair, and disgusting. I had to personally hire carpet cleaners, maids, and needed to bug maintenance to get out fixed. There were $200+ of HIDDEN fees EVERY MONTH. The amenities are always broken and very dirty. No blame to the maintenance staff however, they seem like nice people. On top of that, my apartment was unhealthy to live in. It smelled terrible ALL YEAR and they gaslighted me into thinking it was fine. By the summer, I discovered mold growing on almost all my stuff due to humidity and bad air in the apartment. When I moved, I realized EVERYTHING I OWNED SMELLED-INCLUDING ME. Like other reviews say, they will try to charge you enormous amounts for almost anything- seemingly fictitious and you will have no say ... Just a petty fight with some "management" younger than you. AND THERE WAS NO SOAP IN THE WOMEN'S PUBLIC RESTROOM FOR ALMOST THE ENTIRE YEAR- even after I told them. Half the time, they do not do what they say they will. I know they will read this so I hope they don't take it out on me financially but to some degree, they should understand. They hurt me financially (A LOT), they hurt my health (a lot of nights I couldn't breathe and later I found out it was mold), they disrespected me repeatedly, and also caused me a lot of trouble- and in the end, still seeing how they can milk more money from you... Like you are an object for use. When I saw new students with parents coming in- I wanted so badly to tell them to run as they were signing with a "friendly" staff but I thought it wasn't my place.... Edit: I realized I am still dealing with the health effects of mold right now. I believe I was subject to mycotoxins all year and I'm struggling to fix my health.

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
Don’t move in here! First, you’ll be charged for all sorts of unreasonable things. I really don’t understand why I’m being told I have to pay a $200 cleaning fee when there was no damage, and I had already spent two days cleaning before I left. You’ll never really know what that $200 was supposedly for, it just magically disappears into their pocket.

Fine. I understand that apartments will squeeze every extra fee they possibly can, so I’m not even going to waste my time arguing about that. But what truly amazes me is that when I went back to the office to simply ask a question about my package, the woman working there decided to put on quite a show. She glared at me like I had personally ruined her day, told me it “wasn’t her problem,” cut me off three times before I could even finish my sentence, and made it very clear she had zero interest in helping. So I gave up and left, because clearly, my $1,500 a month in rent was never enough to buy even basic courtesy. Is this what passes for “customer service” here? It’s almost impressive how quickly she managed to remind me that once you’ve moved out, you’re basically invisible, after all, why bother being polite to someone you can’t bill anymore?😅

At this price, you can definitely find a much better apartment and better service in Champaign, so why come here just to be treated badly?

title: Latitude Apartments
text:
I deeply regret signing a lease with Latitude Apartments. After applying, I found myself in a financial predicament and tried to cancel the lease within 72 hours. Contrary to what should have been, they approved my lease within minutes of my cancellation request, which should not have been legally binding. Adding insult to injury, the rent for the identical apartment model was slashed by $176/month just a dozen days after my lease agreement, with no prior notice or offer to adjust my rate. To date, they have dismissed all my reasonable requests.

This entire ordeal has been a massive letdown, and I urge extreme caution to potential tenants. Their lack of transparency and failure to deal fairly has left me with significant regrets about my decision.

text:
I just moved in and the place is pretty nice. Honestly, it exceeded my expectations. I thought my 1-bedroom was going to be too small, but it's actually nice. The Wi-Fi is stupid fast, which is important for me because I work remotely. Additionally, I also appreciate the good air conditioning because my work computer can get hot. So far, the management team has been professional. In particular, Layla, Ciara, and Jasmine were chill. Good job, Latitude.

Edit (3 Stars): I have to change my review to 3-stars. AC isn't working, which not only makes me feel uncomfortable, but my work computer relies on cool air. It's currently 89 degrees in my unit... And the Wifi is suddenly acting peculiar now. I pay over $1600/mo to avoid these issues and so I should expect this stuff to be dealt with as soon as possible.

Despite my disappointment with the amenities, I do want to mention a maintenance gentlemen who works there is really cool.

Edit (1 Star): Definitely don't recommend this place, they randomly raised my rent to nearly $2000/mo when it should've be $1650/mo. I've had over $500 worth of amazon packages stolen, their cameras in the package room apparently doesn't work and the security guard (that I'm paying for) is nonexistent. The hallway smells terrible. The floors are disgusting. I genuinely believe they robbed me

title: Illini Manor Apartments
text:
Lived here for one year.

Pros:
- Right on campus. This works out great if you have classes near the psych building or armory, or you're working at the IKE.
- Renovated. Relatively new apartments. Everything stayed functional with the exception of some power outages here and there. Plenty of sunlight at the southern half of the building.
- In-house washers & dryers. Never had a problem with waiting on others to finish their laundry cycle.
- Static rent rate. No changes in the monthly rent, so if you're concerned about saving money, don't worry about electricity and water.
- Easy rent payment. Just go on the JSM website and pay your rent every month. The application there isn't as intuitive as I'd like it to be, though.
- Maintenance. The maintenance team were always courteous and did their duties.
- Bike racks. There's plenty of bike rack slots by the building.
- Staff. I liked the staff. They were responsive and easy to interact with.
- Amenities. The amenities were to my liking. An AC unit on the big window with vertical curtains/shades that I really liked. Heater worked. The food disposal in the sink was great. The mini fridge was satisfactory. I never took advantage of the JSM VIP program, though.
- Delivery. Never had a problem with missing packages delivered at my door while I was out of the building. This depends on your neighbors, of course.
- Internet. I was quite surprised by the reliable internet service, since it's provided by Pavlov Media. I had a troublesome year prior to this residence with them, but I had few issues with the internet at Illini Manor. There were some occasions where the internet cut out, but only for seconds or a minute (as opposed to days). The speed is *enormous* for what I'm used to. Gratuitous, even.

Cons:
- No solid parking spot. There are meters and there's a loading area in front of the building, but if you want to have any kind of overnight parking, you're going to have to find them through other means. There are streets around that you can buy a permit for the year, but they were all too expensive for me. There's also the C9 parking lot a block away that allows Friday (after 4 or 5 pm) to Sunday free parking, though it'll sometimes get packed. I always had to park way across campus north of Green & east of Lincoln for the majority, unfortunately.
- Bike racks. The design for the bike racks are wonky. I'm pretty sure you're supposed to have the bike over the railing, but they're positioned in such a way that prevents you from even doing that. With heavy wind, my bike would almost always topple over because there's not much blocking it as other bike racks would.
- Minimalism. Little walking space (surprise, surprise). I came in with little material possessions initially, but as the year went by, I had accrued a bit too much and space really became an issue. This shouldn't be a problem if you keep to having few possessions around.
- Frequent showings. By the second half of the spring semester, showings were coming in a lot. This wouldn't be an issue if you applied for the next year, I'd imagine.
- Walls. Sound passes right through the walls from next-door neighbor to next-door neighbor. I could often times make out whole conversations from my neighbor talking moderately loud. Speakers aren't ideal.
- Overnight power outages. Unsure if this was just me, but I'd wake up to find my alarm clock blinking and displaying the wrong time (an indication the power was shut off then restarted) more times than I'd have liked.
- Street noise. This isn't a problem for me, as I'm okay with sleeping through loud ambience. But, if you can't bear partying early in the morning, this will be an issue. You'll be within frat neighborhood.

All-in-all, I liked my experience, just not enough to stick around for another year.
name: Jack Sparrow

title: Illini Manor Apartments
text: I lived here for one semester my senior year at U of I and had a very good experience.  It was clean, quiet, secure, and there was laundry in the building, a sun deck on the roof, and vaccuums you could use for free.  The units are small, but it's a studio and you're in college so it's to be expected.  I never had anyone from the staff come into my unit.

title: Seven07
text:
The property management here is extremely irresponsible. I often see homeless people staying overnight inside the apartment complex, and management never takes any action it feels unsafe.

What’s even more ridiculous is that I’ve already paid for my parking spot, but the parking tag they gave me often doesn’t work, so I can’t even get into the garage. When I went to the front desk for help, they told me, “I can’t offer you any advice.” I honestly can’t believe it — I’ve paid for parking, yet I get locked out of the garage, and they think it’s not their problem? No one tried to help or fix the issue. That’s just absurd.

I have been consistently paying for parking every month and have a valid parking pass. However, the apartment never issued me a required parking sticker. Despite this, the towing company they work with only recognizes the sticker — and my car was almost towed because of this.

Dont‘t rent this apartment
name: Wen Ellen
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Wen,
Thank you for reaching out and sharing your experience. We sincerely appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns and ensure your time at Seven07 is comfortable and stress-free.
We want to reassure you that we have on-site personnel conducting regular patrols throughout the building during all after-hours shifts. We take the safety of our community very seriously and continue to monitor these patrols to maintain a secure environment for all residents.
Regarding parking, we want to ensure your access is seamless. We recently upgraded our entire parking system this year to improve reliability. While the system is currently fully operational and we haven't had reports of issues from other residents, it is possible your specific tag simply needs to be synced with the new software. We have also sent out several email reminders regarding the window stickers required by the towing company to ensure our residents' spots are protected.
Please stop by the front desk or email us at leasing@liveseven07.com so we can personally test your tag, get it synced, and provide you with your required sticker. We are more than happy to help get this resolved for you immediately!
Best regards,
The Seven07 Management Team

title: Seven07
text: Would give 0 stars if I could. This place says they are luxury apartment when in reality they barely do anything for their residents. Often times when I request maintenance, things are not fixed. On top of that I recently found out that the student workers who run the front desk have master keys to every apartment and can just walk in???? I have heard that they will use these master keys to randomly break into residents apartments because they wanted to see the people that live there - which is a complete violation of privacy.  And no, I am not talking about the mandated inspections. I am referring to front desk workers using their master keys in an unprofessional way that is a complete violation of privacy. I do not believe we should be trusting young college students with running an apartment complex. On top of that packages are always lost (as the young college students who work there are always on their phones instead of actually organizing packages). Stay away from Seven07. You will get scammed and you will not feel comfortable or safe living here.
name: Mary J
responseFromOwnerText:
Mary, 

Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns. We take resident feedback and safety very seriously, and we want to clarify a few important points:
Regarding unit access: Student staff do not have unrestricted master key access to apartments. During scheduled unit inspections—which have been communicated weekly for over a month—our student team is assigned specific units on approved inspection lists and is authorised to enter only those units for that purpose. No one is permitted to enter resident units outside of these scheduled and communicated inspections.
Regarding inspections: These inspection rounds are part of our standard building maintenance and safety procedures, and all residents have been notified on a weekly basis for over a month.
Regarding packages:
FedEx and UPS: These carriers deliver directly to resident doors. If a package from FedEx or UPS is missing, residents will need to contact that provider directly using the tracking number.
USPS: USPS deliveries are handled differently. If a package is small enough, the postal carrier may place it directly inside the resident’s mailbox. Larger USPS packages are delivered to the front desk, where we log them and notify residents. If you are unsure, please come see us with your USPS tracking number and we will verify our records.
Regarding phone usage by staff: Our student team also manages our social media presence, which at times requires phone usage to create and post content. This practice is part of their job responsibilities and does not detract from resident service or office professionalism.
Regarding maintenance: If you have any maintenance requests that you feel were not resolved satisfactorily, we encourage you to reach out directly so we can review and follow up promptly.
We care deeply about resident safety, comfort, and trust, and we welcome the opportunity to speak with you privately to ensure your concerns are addressed thoroughly and accurately.
Thank you,
Seven07 Management

title: Seven07
text:
Disappointing First Impression
I recently helped my child move into this complex, and unfortunately, the experience was disappointing, especially considering the price. While the apartments are adequate, they are not as nice as they appear in photos. The unit we moved into had a number of issues, including dirty carpets, mold in the bathroom, and a cheap shower head with hard water stains. Several drawers were also partially broken.

The room itself felt cramped. Although the double bed is a good size, it takes up most of the space. Storage is a major problem—the room has even less than a typical dorm, so you'll definitely need to bring your own. The under-bed drawers are small and don't make good use of the available space, and the "closet" is just a small nook without a door.  The trash shoot are is disgusting, dirty, smelly, sticky, I really doubt anyone cleans it.

The move-in process was also not a great experience. The staff seemed disinterested and acted as if they were doing us a favor.

Hidden Costs & Fees
For such a high price point, you'd expect more transparency and fewer fees. I was surprised to find they nickel and dime you for basic services. They charge a $1.95 fee for ACH/e-check payments, with the only alternative being a paper check. They also charge a separate fee of about $7 to report monthly rent payments for credit reporting. Additionally, most utilities are not included; as far as I can tell, only internet and trash are covered.

Overall, the cost does not align with the quality of the apartment or the service provided. The experience has been frustrating, and I hope this review helps others set their expectations accordingly.
name: Tony V
responseFromOwnerText:
Tony, we’re sorry to hear your first impression didn’t meet expectations. With 548 residents moving in within 48 hours, common areas like trash rooms can take extra time to clear, but we do increase checks during this busy period. Each resident also had a Move-In Condition Form QR code posted on their door so any unit concerns could be documented and addressed right away, and we’d be more than happy to resolve these for you.
We also want to clarify that all fees are disclosed up front in the lease, brochure, and on our website. Internet and trash are included, utilities are billed separately, ACH payments have a $1.95 fee, and our credit reporting program is optional.
We’d love the chance to make this right — please reach us at 217.294.6260 or leasing@liveseven07.com so we can assist further.

title: The Tower at Third
text:
Yes, the staff are helpful. Etc. Etc.

Beyond that, the Tower at Third is an impressive feat of industrial engineering and nothing more.

I’m not quite sure where to even begin. Let's start with the fact that, in my apartment, there was furniture missing from the photos and layouts that were presented to me online as truthful –  or was I to assume to bring my own coat rack, shelves, and bed drawers? I also happen to have friends staying in the Tower and, for the same price, they have different (better) desks than I.

Many people are pointing out the fact that the windows don't open, which is a problem, too, but something else that bothered me was the fact that the bathroom fan doesn't turn on – in fact, it’s not supposed to turn on, according to staff. In fact, almost everything in the apartment is old and decrepit, and there was always at least something wrong.

Beyond the room itself, the corridors, lobby, and about everything else, is a mess. The residents treat this place like a dump and maintenance doesn't seem to care much. It is truly the broken window theory manifest. The tiles on the elevator roofs are constantly torn off, there was oftentimes vomit and other garbage on the elevator floors (of the 2/3 elevators that ever worked), there was cat litter in the lobby that wasn’t cleaned for a few days, etc. The washing machines and drying machines were oftentimes broken, dirty, and didn't properly wash nor dry my clothes, and the fact that you have to pay for these services makes it worse.

But despite these faults, I was keeping myself from posting a review online. That is, until I was sitting down in my apartment, relaxing after a long day of work, ready to put nice, calming music on, and there it was… Red Lion.

When you’re touring the apartment, it may not come to mind the fact that there is a loud nightclub feet away from you. After all, you’re most-likely touring during the club’s non-business hours. You may not regard the fact that, should you sign here, there may be–and there very well is–a constant hum and drum as you try to concentrate on your work, get a good night’s rest, read, or do any sort of leisurely activity. And it’s not just a hum and drum – I can perfectly hear Rhianna’s 2011 smash hit “We Found Love” seeping into the ambiance of Mahler’s Fifth Sympathy. And while it is probably no fault of The Tower's, it does perfectly illustrate the tribulations of living here – the selfish behavior of others, the general cold apathy and disregard that everyone in this establishment and around it has of what you would like to do and how you would like to spend your quiet time, and the constant reminder that you will now and forever be bothered so long as you live at the Tower at Third.

I did not think to write a review, but I came to the realization that it is better not to be silent. I truly do not want you, the reader, to sign here. There are better options.

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re very sorry to hear about the frustrations you’ve experienced and take your concerns seriously. Regarding the study rooms in Building 2, they were temporarily restricted to prevent inappropriate use and non-residents from accessing the spaces, but they are now available 24/7 for residents.

For lease pricing, we operate on a tier system, which means prices can change quickly throughout the year, and specials vary based on the market. We always aim to provide fair pricing compared to similar communities.

We also appreciate your feedback about sidewalk and ramp maintenance, pool access, and other amenities. Resident safety and accessibility are top priorities, and we will review our processes to ensure sidewalks, ramps, and amenities meet the highest standards.

We value your input and encourage you to reach out directly to our management team so we can address your concerns and work toward a better experience for you.

Octave@assetliving.com
217-363-2888

title: Octave
text:
Pros: Location, Parking, Amenities
Cons: Correspondence and communication with the front office - Generally no problems for the past twelve months. But when something came up, and I was looking for someone to be accountable, my opinion is they fell short.  As an example, when my student moved in, we had to pay out-of-pocket for a housecleaner because the kitchen was not move-in ready.
name: Jeff D
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re glad to hear you’ve enjoyed the location, parking, and amenities, but we’re truly sorry to learn that your experience with communication and the move-in condition fell short of expectations.

Regarding the kitchen not being move-in ready, we want to clarify that we do use professional cleaners for all common areas during our mass move-in period. However, if there were residents who renewed and remained in the unit, their continued occupancy may have impacted the condition of shared spaces. If this had been brought to our attention at move-in, we would have been more than happy to send our cleaners back to ensure the kitchen met your standards. We’re very sorry to hear that you felt the need to hire an outside cleaner and spend money unnecessarily.

We truly value your perspective and would appreciate the opportunity to understand more about where we fell short and see if we can help resolve this issue. Please reach out to us and ask to speak with the property manager so we can discuss this further and work toward a positive resolution.

Thank you again for your feedback—we’re here to help.

P: 217-363-2888 
E: Octave@assetliving.com

title: Octave
text:
Bought Reviews: Pay for Luxury, end up cleaning up sewer waste.
This is a warning, DO NOT SIGN HERE.

Do not be deceived by the paid for reviews. This place pretends to be luxurious, but has been nothing but an absolute nightmare to live at.
Octave offers events where they offer free food and coupons for writing positive reviews. There's dozens of reviews from a week ago all from accounts that have only reviewed this place talking about how nice it was.

Now to my awful experience.
I have UC, it's an awful disease, and it makes it so I need 24/7 access to a toilet. And this place having private bathrooms for each room was one of the main reasons I even decided to sign here.
Literally the SECOND DAY after I move in, the private bathroom that I paid for starting leaking constantly from the ceiling. The very next day I went to the front desk told them about it, and they filed my issue under "Electrical" despite it being a very obvious plumbing issue. Their "maintenance team" come in expecting an electrical issue, realize it wasn't and go upstairs to "fix" the issue. Then later that same day, it started downpouring from the ceiling. I go upstairs to talk to the guy, and his toilet is leaking, FECES, URINE, whatever the hell that was in it, leaking all over his floor, and dripping down on mine.
I put in another maintenance request, and they say they "fixed" it again. Then a while later my ceiling starts leaking again. FINALLY they decide to get actual plumbers and not their "maintenance team." Who come in, say we don't know how this is happening. They call me later that day, and say they've identified the issue, and it'll take a week for parts to arrive. I wait two weeks, and my bathroom is still leaking. I was in talks with UIUC's Student Legal Services, and after meeting with the lawyer, they kinda just said "well as long as they're 'doing something'" there's not much we can do.

To summarize: this place is a complete scam, they deceive you to get you to sign, buy fake reviews, and put on a front of luxury; but the second you sign here, and they've secured your money, they could not care less about you.
name: Siyu Xue
responseFromOwnerText:
Siyue,

Thank you for your feedback. We’re truly sorry for the inconvenience caused by the ceiling leak and understand how frustrating that must have been. Please know that we responded to each maintenance request promptly—from replacing the wax ring to bringing in an outside plumbing vendor when the issue continued. Our goal has always been to fully resolve the issue, and to our knowledge, the leak has now been fixed.

We’d also like to clarify that we do not pay for reviews. We host resident events that encourage honest feedback—positive or negative—and we welcome all input.

Our property manager has called and left you a voicemail, as well as sent an email outlining the steps we’ve taken and offering additional support to help improve your experience. If you have any follow-up questions or concerns, we would love to hear from you.

– Octave Apartments Team
217-363-2888 | Octave@assetliving.com

title: Octave
text:
Octave is a nice place to live. One lesson I've learned is that if you want the best information, talk to Braydon (property manager). Here are my experiences from the only 2 times I've interacted with the Octave staff:

1. The filter status on the water fountain was red, and I asked several times for that to be fixed. The students at the front desk told me that it's "not their job" since there's an external cleaning company contracted for these types of jobs. Many months later, after my parents sternly spoke with the front desk, it was finally fixed.

2. The garbage trucks come between 3-7 AM, so if your window faces the alley in bldg 1, it's guaranteed to wake you up. The front desk staff had "no clue when the garbage truck came" or what to do about it. They said there's nothing they can do.

What I learned from Braydon is that he orders the parts and that he had actually ordered the part but since there were many vendors involved, it was taking a while. He also explained that there is an external contract with the garbage truck companies (Republic Services & Midwest Fiber Recycling) and that there's absolutely nothing he can do about it as the property manager of Octave. In short, the people at the front desk don't give much help and give responses that might make you feel insulted as a paying resident
name: Avaneesh Gokhale
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for your thoughtful review and for sharing your experience. We're glad to hear you enjoy living at Octave and appreciate your kind words about Braydenn—he's truly dedicated to supporting our residents.

We're sorry to hear your concerns weren’t addressed more effectively at first. We understand how frustrating that can be, and we’re actively working on improving communication and service at all levels of the team.

We value your feedback and are committed to making your experience the best it can be.

– The Octave Apartments Team
P: 217-363-2888
E: Octave@assetliving.com

text:

Walls and floors are paper thin, i can hear people walking in the hallway from my desk that's across the apartment. I can hear the wind blowing as well as cars that drive by as well from two streets down.
name: Eric Zhang
responseFromOwnerText:
Eric,

Thank you for your feedback. We understand your frustration and apologize for the room assignment issues. While we do our best to honor preferences, room selections are based on who signs first, and occasionally there are overlapping requests. Now that everyone is moved in, we’d be happy to help with a room switch if all roommates agree.

We also appreciate your comments about sound — while some building elements are out of our control, we’re always looking for ways to improve. Please don’t hesitate to reach out so we can assist further. Please give us a call if you have any follow up questions or concerns at 217-363-2888 or you can email us at Octave@assetliving.com
